There's another story about a princess on a mountain and the sultan wanted her for marriage but she gave him a list of impossible stuff to collect before she can marry her.

One of those things is 300 hundred mosquitoes heart
oh puteri gunung ledang
The moral of the story is that the Sultan was too oblivious that the princess was actually rejecting him by giving him impossible feats to accomplish

- A golden walkway for her to walk to Malacca from the mountain,
- A silver walkway for her to return from Malacca to the mountain,
- Seven barrels of tears
- Seven barrels of young betel nut juices from the betel tree (Areca catechu) also for her to bathe in,
- Seven trays filled with hearts of germs,
- Seven trays filled with hearts of mosquitoes, and
- A bowl of the blood of the Sultan's young son and gold

Prince Serapi fell in love with both of them and wanted both to become his wives. Santubong and Sejinjang both loved Prince Serapi, and a frightful fight ensued since each of them wished to become Prince Serapi’s only wife. The quarrel turned increasingly violent, with Sejinjang hitting Santubong in the cheek with her rice pounder and Santubong fighting back and hitting Sejinjang with her ‘belidak’, a tool used to weave cloths.

When their father, the King, realized what was happening, he was furious. He came down to earth to curse the three of them. Both sisters were transformed into mountains, namely Mount Santubong and Mount Sejinjang, while the broken pieces of Sejinjang’s head scattered into the sea and became islands: Kerak island, Satang island, Talang-Talang island, Lakir island and many more. Prince Serapi was also cursed. He was turned into Mount Serapi, located far far away from the two princesses.

Once upon a time there was a shark named Sura and a crocodile named Baya
They would fight often because of their greed
I mean uh they often fought for their food
Shit I forgot what happens next hang on
One day Baya tells him he’s had enough of all the fighting
Sura agrees
So he suggests that they mark their own territories and hunt for their own food in their own zones
Lest there will be punishment
So Baya agrees
Sura gets the sea, Baya gets the river
baya was robbed
justice for Baya
Well funnily enough…
we all know croc is cooler than shark
So months go by without a fight between either of them

lying

embeds.title: River shark
embeds.description: Glyphis is a genus in the family Carcharhinidae, commonly known as the river sharks. They live in rivers or coastal regions in and around south-east Asia, Africa and parts of Australia.

content: River sharks sounds like a worldbuilder attempt at some silly detail
At some point Baya got suspicious, he realizes that he’s seeing less and less of his prey
Brb
given the biodiversity of the region im not surprised we have silly shit like that
damn nova left to literally rp baya
maybe baya is observant but sura is just a fucking ninja
stealth shark
leaves no trace
Alright I’m back
So of course he suspects it’s Sura’s doing
So Baya concocts a plan
He injured a deer and put it on the edge of a river
And he hid himself
Sura saw the deer and tried to take it to the sea but Baya caught him red-handed
Baya basically calls Sura a greedy mf since he has plenty of fish in the sea
So they fought again
It was apparently very brutal
GET ITS ASS
Baya got his tail bitten which made it always curve to the left
Sura’s tail also got bitten to the point of almost breaking off
In the end Sura went back to the sea
Baya feels proud of defending his territory
Yeah that’s it
is this like doomed yaoi or something
Heheh
Anyway that’s the lore of my city’s symbol

ok so once upon a time when singapore was a malay kingdom
there was a swordfish problem
it was real bad
As a reference to our coat of arms
so the sultan was looking for solutions
enter nameless boy whose name i forgot

anyway he had a great solution
build wooden fence around all the water bodies
the swordfish leap up and get their noses stuck in the fence
this worked
and they spent days just cleaning up all the swordfish stuck in the fences

they killed so many swordfish that they stained the soil of singapore red
but problem solved
anyway after that the sultan figured that the boy was a national security risk

so he had the boy executed on the same hill
and thats why bukit merah in singapore is named that :)

The MC is part of a special university that focuses on investigating the supernatural. They're a "Keeper", a person with high spiritual energy who can be used to supply Awakeners, supernatural spirits (often originating from dead humans) with energy so they can fight, by linking with them.

The supernatural spirits all have a core of Silver, a magical substance. The prologue starts with something going wrong at the protagonist's awakening ceremony and they end up forming contracts with Ramona, their long time friend, the young (well, ancient, but he died as a kid) knight Ogier, the rampaging awakener Lotan and their young Awakener professor, doll. It turns out it was because Tawil, a spirit the university keeps in their basement, was also going berserk, but after a fight, she touches MC's cheek and calms down. Tawil also looks exactly like Ramona.
Aigis
Yeahhh i remeber her looking like ramona
Is “they” the mc?

In the first chapter, ||MC and Ramona investigate a workhouse that takes in orphans, because a university investigator has gone missing there. A young thief named Jenkins is also looking into it to find her missing sister. It turns out that, unbeknownst to the workhouse owner, a nun named Sarah, a group named the Sculptor's Association has been experimenting on the people she's taken in. She thinks they're just doctors. They killed the missing investigator and turned his Silver Core into a cursed magical eye that they put into a small orphan named Aigis. The eye turns everything it looks at into candy, including a boy who Aigis was really good friends with. At the end of the story, Aigis takes her own life to no longer hurt people. But she leaves behind a Silver Core and so while she will now never grow up, she can at least have some kind of life as an Awakener. But she loses all her memories, and the MC decides to not tell Jenkins until she gets all her memories back.
Also, another good friend of the MC named William (their super awesome senior, did you know he smells faintly of cedar) goes to investigate the Sculptor's Association but he goes missing.||

She's a major character in chapter 2, but not really beyond that. (Chapter 2 Spoilers) ||She's actually Jenkins' sister Mason, who has been experimented on by the Sculptors and gained a split personality as a result. I think she's also not dead, but, y'know, not really human anymore either||
||Not because of the DID, tbc, more because of what they did to her body||
Whats DID
Alive people have powers?
Like pollux and 24?
Dissociative identity disorder. She doesn't REALLY have it per se, but she functions similarly and parts of the game use similar terminology for how her condition works (such as "fronting" or "host")
I see

The alive Awakeners still have powers, yes. Every Awakener, alive or dead, has some kind of a Lovecraftian power source. In Pollux's case, he was already born inhuman and then had a magical heart inserted into him. 24 was experimented on and, presumably, sewn together with some inhuman beings
Okkk

Yeah, I'm saying DID because "fronting"/"host" is plural terminology and her frenzy translation uses that word

Oh, and I just saw this: Most people can't see the dissolution and they don't know it exists, this includes the creatures. They just see people that are kind of sick. The museum is an especially dangerous place because the people who own it like to experiment with dissolution stuff and that also affects the surrounding area.
There are whole towns that have been wiped out by dissolution (see Aurita's sidestory), but nobody remembers them.
However, larger cities are likelier to have people who can see and fight dissolution entities, such as the Mythag staff.
Also, dissolution has been getting much worse recently, it isn't usually this bad.
consider: gpollux and gcastor, but the only difference from normal pollux and castor is that they have tail feathers and are thus like 20% more bird
